title: Why did my electricity bill increase?
text: Your electricity bill may increase for several reasons. First, higher energy consumption is the most common cause. You may have used more kilowatt-hours (kWh) this billing period compared to the previous one. This can happen due to seasonal changes (heating in winter, air conditioning in summer), the addition of new appliances, working from home more frequently, or having more people in the household. Second, tariff rate adjustments may apply. Regulated energy tariffs are reviewed periodically by the national energy regulator. If wholesale energy market prices have risen, your supplier may adjust rates accordingly. Third, regulated charges such as network fees and levies may have increased. These are set by the regulator and are outside your supplier's control. Fourth, an estimated bill followed by an actual meter read can cause a catch-up charge if previous estimates were too low. To understand your specific increase, compare the kWh consumed this period against the previous period first, then check if any line-item rates have changed. Your billing history showing the last 3 bills is available in your customer portal.
